2026-03-09 23:23:18,"[2152, 1673, 312, 479, 941, 259, 1994, 157, 2966, 1813, 572, 2504, 3024, 762, 1638, 518]",Can/Does VSCode handle system shutdown correctly?,"I'm not sure if this is a feature request, or a bug, or if its even doable, or if my underlying assumptions are correct. Apologies.
Today I had accidentally clicked on ""restart"" on my PC's start menu (I had meant to restart a machine I had RDP'd into).
Luckily I had notepad opened with some unsaved changes and it blocked the restart enough for me to cancel the restart process.

However VSCode was open the entire time and when I came back it was in a broken state: clicking on a reference in my C# code resulted in a message `actual command NOT FOUND`; there was a notification that the TypeScript service had shutdown after restarting 5 times and would not be restarted, etc.

My theory is that Windows tried to shutdown these helper processes individually but had not yet sent the VSCode main process the system shutdown message, and continuously sending shutdown to those helper processes that VSCode tries to start up in response to them being shutdown (or blocking their startup altogether).

I don't think Windows sends a ""system is shutting down"" message to processes in a deterministic order, so would it be difficult to find out what message triggered the shutdown? I guess that would only be possible if your helpers are all hosted under some VSCode process. If it is possible the feature request would be for the helper processes to be required to forward the ""windows is shutting down message"" to the VSCode main process, so that it can then not only stop trying to respawn processes but can also start shutting itself down properly.

column1: 2026-03-09 23:23:21,"[2152, 1673, 312, 479, 941, 259, 1994, 157, 2966, 1813, 572, 2504, 3024, 762, 1638, 518]",Improved visibility of high CPU extensions / language servers,"Lately while I'm using VSCode it's basically always 100% CPU. Pretty annoying. I have a few projects open but nothing extreme (I'm not editing the Typescript compiler for example). If I check in `top` the culprit is almost always `Code Helper (Renderer)`. Not very useful.
Using `htop` or similar you can see the full command, e.g. the one that is currently at 100% CPU for me is Pylance:
However I have observer 100% CPU with basically all languages I use: Rust-analyzer, Pylance, cpptools and ESlint/Typescript. I'm guessing they're just buggy.

It's probably infeasible to fix all of those extensions, but it would be nice if VSCode at least worked around their bugginess. Currently the only effort it goes to here is the Process Explorer, but it usually doesn't even show these processes!
No mention of pylance there! In fact it's not even showing the right window - I have several open but it's showing some random window that isn't even focused. It should surely show all windows?
1. Fix the Process Explorer so it actually shows all windows & processes
2. Add a dedicated CPU usage view for language servers/extensions.
3. If a language server/extension is detected as using 100% CPU for more than a certain amount of time, show a notification with an offer to restart it.
4. If possible, add an option to throttle language servers/extensions (e.g. using `nice`).",feature,feature

column1: Does this issue occur when all extensions are disabled?: Yes

There seems to be a mismatch between the action name (`editor.action.selectHighlights`) and its textual description (Select All Occurrences of Find Match). The behaviour seems to correspond to the latter.

For background, I am using a Haskell LSP plugin. When my cursor is on a variable or function name, all occurrences in the file are highlighted. This is the same information I get from the references view. But when I call `selectHighlights`, the editor seems to do a dumb text search - i.e. other entities, in other scopes, with the same name, are also selected. This is useless.
So is there actually any way to select all currently highlighted strings in the editor?",feature,bug

With every new release, dozens of new configuration options are added to VSCode, as well as new extensions being released on a regular basis. It is now really hard to even try to stay on top of it, without knowing if this or that feature could potentially lead to a productivity boost.

In larger repos/workspaces with complex configurations, it is becoming a productivity cost to not configure everything well, while leaving some space for individuals to customise VSCode to their own specific flavour. So I'm wondering if anyone has thought of, or is providing, consulting services to help teams get to the ideal configuration?

Getting 1-10% extra productivity from each developer because your IntelliSense always displays the right suggestions is a simple yet very rewarding idea, with real value, and we'd thereforce be willing to pay someone to help us get it right. For example, I guess that IntelliSense gives us proper recommendation 60-80% of the time as of now.
I'm sure there are other things we don't know, like hidden gems in the extensions marketplace that could help us too.
I know this is not the perfect place, but maybe other people have thoughts to share on this, and someone with the right experience could suddenly think about giving this a go :)",question,question
1.  Open cmd in the Insiders directory ""C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft VS Code Insiders""
2.  Type `""Code - Insiders.exe"" --list-extensions`. This doesn't list any extension, but starts VSCode Insiders.
3. Typing `code --list-extensions` in the same command prompt produces a list of installed extensions in release version of VSCode.
3. Typing `""Code - Insiders.exe"" --install-extension ms-vscode.cpptools` just started VSCode Insiders and doesn't install the extension.

4. After installing ms-vscode.cpptools inside VSCode Insiders, typing `""Code - Insiders.exe"" --uninstall-extension ms-vscode.cpptools` just starts VSCode Insider and doesn't uninstall the extension.",question,bug

2026-03-09 23:23:51,"[2152, 1673, 312, 479, 941, 259, 1994, 157, 2966, 1813, 572, 2504, 3024, 762, 1638, 518]",i have install nodejs and when i was trying to create an react project i was getting this error,"I have installed nodejs and i was trying to create an react app by using this command npx create-react-app appname
but i am getting an error which,goes like this
Creating a new React app in C:\Users\ACER\desktop\reactCourse\my-app.
Installing packages. This might take a couple of minutes.
Installing react, react-dom, and react-scripts with cra-template...
Unexpected error. Please report it as a bug:

Currently you are unable to specify installation paths for node modules used by Code via `scripts/npm.sh`, only the architecture. This is an issue for package management systems on Linux where specific packages may ""own"" a set of files or directories, and thus produces conflicts on the system when multiple packages attempt to own the same set of files.
I'm proposing the following options be added to `npm.sh`:
`--installdir=DIR`: This would enable you to specify a general installation directory:
- Beneficial for build systems that use isolated install directories (where the contents of it are then considered the contents of a binary package) away from the build directories.
`--node-modules=DIR`: This would enable you to specify **the root** of where you want node modules to be installed.
- Resolves potential file conflicts for packages and pollution of global node_modules.

`--installdir=/var/ypkg-root/install/vscode/`: This would be the root of the file system. Installation of binaries such as those placed in `/usr/bin` would begin with the installdir, so it is conceivable that the directory would for the binaries would then be `/var/ypkg-root/install/vscode/usr/bin`.
- Note: This would only be on the isolated build system, the final contents installed on the user's system would still be /usr/bin, this would just specify where to install things on build-time.

`--node-modules=$installdir/usr/share/vscode/`: This would install node modules to a `node_module` folder in `/usr/share/vscode`. This keeps it isolated from any `npm` or `nodejs` system packages and files. Atom does something similar, with installing things to (_for example_) `/usr/share/atom/resources/...`
4. Run any PowerShell in the original PowerShell Window

2026-03-09 23:23:58,"[2152, 1673, 312, 479, 941, 259, 1994, 157, 2966, 1813, 572, 2504, 3024, 762, 1638, 518]",vscode.window.showTextDocument loads file in editor but Thenable calls onrejected when loading multiple files,"In my extension FileGroup a user reported an issue: rioj7/fileGroup#6 where the files get loaded in editors but he gets error messages for each file with the text: `Error: Could NOT open editor for ""file:///d%3A/dir1/dir2/file.ext"".`
All files exist but still a `Could NOT open editor for ` error message is generated.
* If he wants to load 1 file the error message does not happen
* If he wants to load 2 files the error messages **sometimes does NOT** happen
* If he wants to load 3+ files the error messages (most likely) always happen
A slimmed down version of the `extention.js` (before the workaround) is:
const openFile = filePath => new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
vscode.window.showTextDocument(vscode.Uri.file(filePath), { preview: false, viewColumn: column }).then( editor => { resolve(true); },
I have wrapped `vscode.window.showTextDocument` in a `Promise` because I do not expect that a `Thenable` will work with `Promise.all()`.

The question is why is the `onrejected` callback of `.then(onfulfilled, onrejeted)` called when the file is loaded in an editor. It only happens when multiple file load Promises are created and waited for. If the function `vscode.window.showTextDocument` performs correct the `.then()` should call the `onfulfilled` callback (no error message).

Can there be some ""race"" condition internally where multiple calls to `vscode.window.showTextDocument` manipulate some global data that is not guarded by some ""mutex""? Because with 2 files it sometimes happens.

column1: 2026-03-09 23:24:19,"[2152, 1673, 312, 479, 941, 259, 1994, 157, 2966, 1813, 572, 2504, 3024, 762, 1638, 518]",fixing c++ bugs in javascript -,"Emscripten is a very good project to port c/c++ projects to javascript,
there is a feature called Manual print debugging, which prints out the line number of the given c++ code in the ported javascript code.
My feature request sounds crazy, but there is a valid reason.
Because of emscripten you can debug ported javascript code from c++,
what i would like to achieve, to make changes in javascript and automatically converted back to c++.
I think that functionality can empower the free source world.
Especially when you have a badly written aging project like ffmpeg and you would like to find bugs in it,
so you can use emscripten debug it in the browser and convert back the changes into c++.
So any freesource project which misses manpower can get free hands quickly as javascript is a well known and easy to use language for everyone.

There are special cases, but to check and validate an encoder algorithm make it more efficient programatically can be a valid reason to use that technic. If you would be able to read the algorithm in a better way and debug in a better way, you can get an idea to write far better algorithm than the heavily patented mpeg h264 and aac. (I think it's a common problem in every browsers nowadays)

Not to mention c++ projects, which has been ended because lots of resources was needed to fix bugs further. (Osmosis is a good example on open street map - everybody is using it as a google alternative, but the osmisis itself is in 0.55 version, and finished the development after 2016)",question,feature

> [Instead please open feature requests for individual cases you'd like to see supported. For a long running operation for example, typically extensions would hand VS Code a long running task and then we would figure out how to present process status to the user. That way it's consistent across extensions and languages](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/146593#issuecomment-1086371853)
How do you feel about letting extension developers signal to VSCode that a particular action is important?
This is a very, very rough draft of what this might look like.
// The mouse cursor or pointer in VSCode
// is a ""spinner"" while this command is in process
// as a subscript on the cursor, temporarily
// Displays a green check mark, like ✔,
// as a subscript on the cursor, temporarily

There is a feature of vscode that caches unsaved documents after closing the editor.  When the editor is reopened the unsaved file is open within the editor giving the user a chance to save it.

This is a useful feature but its quite jarring when opening an instance of vscode via the _Open with Code_ commands as this forces two windows to be opened at the same time.  This may also occur when starting an instance of vscode via the command line + arguments.

column1: 2026-03-09 23:24:47,"[2152, 1673, 312, 479, 941, 259, 1994, 157, 2966, 1813, 572, 2504, 3024, 762, 1638, 518]","Need scrollTop, scrollBottom, and scrollToCursor commands for Home, End and Control-L key bindings on macOS","Hi there,

If we follow Apple apps like xcode or Pages as an example for macOS text input key mappings, some things are fine in VS Code. ```cmd+up``` is mapped to ```cursorTop``` and ```cmd+down``` is mapped to ```cursorBottom``` by default which is great. We can map ```pageup``` to ```scrollPageUp``` and ```pagedown``` to ```scrollPageDown``` to follow macOS behaviour of not moving the cursor when these are pressed (PS should these be macOS defaults?).

But we cannot map ```home``` to ```scrollTop``` and ```end``` to ```scrollBottom``` because the commands ```scrollTop``` and ```scrollBottom``` do not exist! Therefore on my keyboard ```home``` and ```end``` do nothing in a text file.
Could these missing two commands please be added to allow standard macOS key mappings for ```home``` and ```end```?

PS: After scrolling one often uses ```Ctrl-L``` ([reference](https://support.apple.com/en-in/HT201236)) to return to the cursor position - would be great if suitable command for this standard macOS shortcut was also added, e.g. ```scrollToCursor```",question,feature

1. Open a new window from an existing window.

This is related to: New windows should get the size of the previous active window #10864. It's great that the new setting (""window.newWindowDimensions"": ""inherit"") was added to preserve the window size.

But, the new window overlaps exactly on the existing window. It might be a little bit confusing. A user may think that the previous window was closed. Or, was replaced by a new one. I think it should follow the behavior of widely used applications such as Windows Explorer or Firefox, both of which cascade the new window a little bit so as to differentiate it with the previous one.
Probably a new configuration setting such as, ""window.newWindowPosition"" with options, ""same"" and ""cascade"" could be added.",feature,feature

2026-03-09 23:25:07,"[2152, 1673, 312, 479, 941, 259, 1994, 157, 2966, 1813, 572, 2504, 3024, 762, 1638, 518]","In the Find/Replace widget, labels for the ""Replace"" and ""Replace All"" buttons don't show keybindings","In the Find widget, the ""Previous match"" and ""Next match"" buttons have labels which include their associated keybindings in parentheses. The ""Replace"" and ""Replace All"" buttons in the Replace Input box don't display keyboard shortcuts.

I looked into this a bit before creating an issue, and the solution isn't the simple ""look up keybinding --> add to label"" approach that I hoped for. It looks like Replace and Replace All don't actually have keybindings defined. Instead, the keys are hardcoded into the `_onReplaceInputKeyDown()` method of [findWidget.ts](https://github.com/Microsoft/vscode/blob/85de39a54d78de5d247b57d1e2c50cc81192212e/src/vs/editor/contrib/find/browser/findWidget.ts#L284-L292).

I would like to see Replace and Replace All get configurable keyboard shortcuts. The defaults would come from the existing hardcoded values, so the core functionality would be unchanged. With configurable keyboard shortcuts it would be easier to display them cleanly on the Replace and Replace All buttons, and we get some flexibility as a free bonus.

I would like to implement this and submit a PR, but wanted to document and perhaps discuss the issue first to make sure that sort of contribution would be OK. Some comments in #406 brushed up against what I'm suggesting here, but I didn't find any other potential duplicate issues.",feature,feature

2026-03-09 23:25:07,"[2152, 1673, 312, 479, 941, 259, 1994, 157, 2966, 1813, 572, 2504, 3024, 762, 1638, 518]",Shortkey Presenter,"Similar to the [Presentation Assistant Plugin](https://plugins.jetbrains.com/plugin/7345-presentation-assistant) from IntelliJ I'd like to show my peers the shortcuts I'm using while working. That plugin shows overlays of the shortcuts you use, when in presentation mode. It differentiates itself from plugins that teach _you_ shortcuts, by showing every shortcut that you did use (e.g. Cmd+Shift+P), in contrast to the ones that you didn't use (e.g. click on debug).

I've seen that listening to events was [decided against](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/78091#issuecomment-518545491). Therefore this can't be added as a plugin. If you register a plugin for all the commands, then you run into endless loops trying to show a message and then invoking the command.
[Therefore I suggest](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/issues/78091#issuecomment-521640092) an opt-in feature, which displays the command alongside the used shortcut.
I am happy to help with this, if it's something you find valuable for the core. I would need some guidance on which files are relevant for this, too.",question,feature

2026-03-09 23:25:09,"[2152, 1673, 312, 479, 941, 259, 1994, 157, 2966, 1813, 572, 2504, 3024, 762, 1638, 518]","Allow to configure vertical scrollbar width for terminal, explorer pane, preview pane","Using VSCode 1.58.2 on Ubuntu 20.04.2, Gnome 3.36.8
With version 1.58 it is possible to set the width of the scrollbar, which is great [Editor scroll bar customization](https://code.visualstudio.com/updates/v1_58#_editor-scroll-bar-customization).

However, the width of the scrollbar is still not configurable in the Explorer pane, the integrated terminal and the Markdown/Asciidoc preview. When the monitor has resolution greater than 1920x1080, it is rather difficult to grad the scrollbar. Especially with GTK3 theme where the mouse pointer is not very accurate close to the edge. Actually, it is almost impossible to grab the scrollbar. Because when the mouse is close to the edge, GTK3 theme thinks that you want to resize the window. So the mouse cursor switches to the resize window function rather than the pointy mouse cursor to allow you to grab the scrollbar.
The issue is similar on the Explorer pane with the vertical scrollbar. Although not absurdly as impossible as with the terminal scrollbar but still quite sensitive to mis-click.

Maybe this is a GTK3 design issue. Nevertheless, the issue seems to only affect VSCode. All other apps whether GTK2 or GTK3 having vertical scrollbar width comparable to that of VSCode works perfectly. I hope this could be fixed in VSCode by:
- Either review the accuracy of mouse pointer detection when close to the right edge. VSCode should not confuse grabbing the scrollbar with resizing the window width.
- OR to configure the width of the vertical scrollbar. To make it simple, one single config setting for these 3 windows enough (explorer, terminal, markdown/asciidoc preview).

2026-03-09 23:25:28,"[2152, 1673, 312, 479, 941, 259, 1994, 157, 2966, 1813, 572, 2504, 3024, 762, 1638, 518]","Explicit keyboard binding when clause context for ""Terminal in editor area""","Is there an explicit keyboard binding when clause context to assign different key bindings to focused integrated & ""in editor area"" terminals?
If not, I'd really like to see them added. Something like `terminalEditorFocus` in addition to the already existing `terminalFocus`.
I have assigned `Ctrl+F4` to `workbench.action.terminal.kill` when `terminalFocus` and to `workbench.action.closeActiveEditor` as a fallback (when no terminal has focus).

However, the `terminalFocus` when clause is also true when an ""in editor area terminal"" has focus for which the `workbench.action.terminal.kill` doesn't work since a dedicated `workbench.action.terminal.killEditor` exists.
In other words, I'd need the following 3 key bindings:
* `Ctrl+F4` -> `workbench.action.terminal.killEditor` when `terminalEditorFocus` (not possible due to missing when context)

2026-03-09 23:25:28,"[2152, 1673, 312, 479, 941, 259, 1994, 157, 2966, 1813, 572, 2504, 3024, 762, 1638, 518]",Toggle auto-format on save per-language,"It would be nice there was a way to enable or disable auto-format on save per-language. For example:
- In general, I want to auto-format on save for languages such as TypeScript that have decent formatters (whose style I agree with)
- For some languages, such as SCSS and CSS, I haven't found a formatter whose style agrees with mine, so I don't want to always auto-format on save in those languages.

- BUT in those same languages, sometimes I'm looking at some code with absolutely no formatting (for example, blob of CSS styles copied from browser debugger), and any formatting style is preferable to the total lack of formatting.
In the last case, I have to enable an extension, format my code, and then re-disable the extension, which is kind of a pain.

(If someone can point me to the right place, I might be interested in implementing this. Also, apologies if this has been requested already--tried to search but may not have used the right terms.)",question,feature
